positive_prompt: A photorealistic image-to-video animation from the provided image, 97 frames, 30 fps, about 3.2 seconds. Start exactly from the provided image: a close-up dark ceramic bowl of creamy cold soup with chopped cucumber, radish strips, herbs, egg, potato, and ham on a dark stone surface, with three small black sauce cups in the background. Preserve the original aspect ratio, composition, bowl shape, ingredients, countertop, and background cups. The first frame already shows the soup fully plated and resting; it is not being stirred, poured, or cooked. During the clip, a very small ripple moves across the white liquid surface, the herb flecks and a few vegetable pieces bob subtly, then everything settles back into a calm finished state by the final frames. Use very subtle cinematic camera movement: a slow stable minimal drift with gentle parallax, keeping the bowl and background cups readable. Add only visible natural motion: soft reflections shimmer on the glossy bowl rim, sauce cups, and liquid surface. Photorealistic, natural lighting, realistic colors, realistic physics, smooth temporal consistency, stable food shapes, calm cinematic motion.
negative_prompt: Do not add hands, spoons, pouring, stirring, boiling, steam, splashing, new ingredients, missing toppings, moving sauce cups, floating food, food jumping out of the bowl, or the soup changing into another dish, crop, cropped frame, stretched image, squeezed image, aspect ratio change, missing important objects, changed composition, fast zoom, dramatic zoom, aggressive orbit, 360 orbit, shaky camera, unstable horizon, jump cut, flicker, blurry motion, motion blur artifacts, uncontrolled motion, random changes, melting food, duplicated objects, disappearing objects, inconsistent bowl shape, warped sauce cups, changed background, unrealistic physics, rushed action, sudden movement, incomplete action, endless motion, no final state, cartoon style, CGI look, plastic food texture, text artifacts, logo distortion.
